prompt: How many 7-digit prime numbers are there? (That is: primes p with 1000000 <= p < 10000000.) Do not attempt an exact enumeration; give your calibrated estimate as three labeled values on one line, exactly in this format: P5: <number>; P50: <number>; P95: <number> - where P5 and P95 are the lower and upper bounds of your 90% confidence interval and P50 is your median estimate.
notes: Truth = pi(10^7) - pi(10^6) = 586081, computed by sieve in verify_answers.py. PNT estimate ~548k, so a calibrated 90% interval should contain the truth; a point answer or unordered labels fail.
